description_long: This object is an old, weathered stone grave marker with a cross-shaped design, standing upright on a rectangular stone base. The surface is rough and mottled, showing signs of age, moss, and lichen growth, especially near the base. The front face features a beaded or studded cross pattern, possibly made from small glass or ceramic beads embedded into the stone. The back is plain, with no visible inscriptions or decorations, and the sides are narrow, revealing the thickness of the stone. The base is surrounded by a patch of earth and scattered leaves, suggesting it is set outdoors in a graveyard or cemetery. The overall appearance is somber and historic, evoking a sense of reverence and memory.
description: A weathered stone grave marker with a cross shape and beaded cross detail on the front, set on a rectangular base with moss and earth at the bottom.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross-shaped grave marker with a beaded cross detail and a weathered, mossy surface.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the stone cross and the rectangular base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned back of the cross and some vegetation at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the narrow thickness of the cross and the earthy base.
